01

About

I'm an Electrical Engineering student at the University of Maryland with a 3.7 GPA, focused on hardware design and chip architecture. My long-term goal is to work in the semiconductor industry in digital chip design and VLSI.

Currently looking for internship opportunities in hardware engineering and embedded systems, as well as undergraduate research opportunities in chip design and hardware architecture labs.

I've been building hands-on experience through Verilog RTL design, embedded systems, and faculty-mentored ML research through UMD's FIRE program. I believe in learning by building real things.

02

Projects

PWM RGB LED Controller

Multi-channel PWM controller in Verilog with FSM-based automatic color sequencing across 4 states. Verified with an automated self-checking testbench and waveform analysis in WaveTrace.

Verilog FSM RTL Verification Icarus Verilog
↗
Outdoor Terrestrial Vehicle

Autonomous fire-suppression robot built with a team of 8. Programmed independent control of 4 mecanum wheels in C++ enabling omnidirectional navigation in a 4×2m arena on a $320 budget.

Arduino C++ Motor Control Embedded Systems
Arduino Ambient Lighting System

Automated lighting system using an LDR sensor and a custom voltage divider circuit. ADC-based ambient monitoring with calibrated C++ threshold control logic for stable, reliable operation.

C++ Arduino ADC Circuit Design
